Title: Strategic enhancement of genetic gain for nutraceutical development in buckwheat: A genomics-driven perspective

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antihypertensive and other nutraceutical effects
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates nuclear import of MYB repressors and thereby contributes to repression of rutin accumulation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Importin beta-like transport factor; SAD2/transport-related protein
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy root culture; protein interaction assay; functional characterization in transgenic system
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtSAD2 interacts with the R2R3-MYB repressor FtMYB11 through its SID-like motif. 2. FtSAD2 mediates transport of FtMYB11 into the nucleus. 3. Through this transport function, FtSAD2 contributes to repression of rutin biosynthesis/accumulation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA-responsive context discussed for related MYBs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction with FtMYB13/14/15 depends on conserved SID motif; FtMYB16 lacks SID motif and does not interact with FtSAD2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate nucleocytoplasmic trafficking of flavonoid repressors in metabolic engineering for higher rutin.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly regulates nucleocytoplasmic trafficking of FtMYB16, a repressor of rutin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy root culture; functional characterization of trafficking signaling pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ftimportin-α1 directly regulates nucleocytoplasmic trafficking of cargo protein FtMYB16. 2. FtMYB16 lacks a SID motif unlike other repressing MYBs. 3. This transport pathway is linked to regulation of rutin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as a trafficking regulator distinct from FtSAD2-dependent transport of SID-containing MYBs.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for engineering flavonoid accumulation by modifying nuclear transport of repressors.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for rutin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to form chalcone scaffold for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: High-throughput RNA sequencing correlation with rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FeCHS was identified among DEGs associated with rutin content differences. 2. It belongs to the flavonoid pathway contributing precursor formation for rutin biosynthesis. 3. Expression was profiled in stem, root and seed of contrasting genotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with higher flavonoid/rutin biosynthetic capacity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed in review as DEG from transcriptomic study; causal functional validation not detailed in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme gene for marker development and pathway engineering to increase rutin.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms early flavanone precursor for rutin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chalcone to flavanone in early flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: High-throughput RNA sequencing correlation with rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FeCHI1 was identified as a differentially expressed flavonoid gene linked with rutin content. 2. It functions early in the pathway leading to rutin accumulation. 3. Expression differences were observed among tissues/genotypes with contrasting rutin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene symbol appears in table of DEGs only; no direct biochemical assay described in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for genomics-assisted improvement of flavonoid content.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for rutin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: High-throughput RNA sequencing correlation with rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FePAL was among DEGs linked to rutin content variation. 2. It provides entry into phenylpropanoid metabolism feeding flavonoid synthesis. 3. Differential expression suggests contribution to nutraceutical flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered rutin biosynthetic potential
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as transcriptomic candidate rather than experimentally proven causal gene in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream engineering target to boost total phenylpropanoid flux.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies p-coumarate precursor pool for rutin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: High-throughput RNA sequencing correlation with rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FeC4H was identified as a DEG linked with rutin content. 2. It contributes upstream precursor formation for flavonoid biosynthesis. 3. Transcript abundance differed among tissues/genotypes associated with rutin variation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with precursor supply for rutin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review cites this gene from DEG list only.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving precursor flux in rutin biofortification.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms dihydroflavonol precursor for rutin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanone to dihydroflavonol en route to flavonol synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: High-throughput RNA sequencing correlation with rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FeF3H was detected among rutin-associated DEGs. 2. It provides mid-pathway conversion toward flavonol/rutin biosynthesis. 3. Expression differences suggest involvement in tissue- and genotype-specific rutin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct validation detailed in review beyond DEG identification.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway gene for enhancing rutin production.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms quercetin/flavonol precursor immediately upstream of rutin glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols in the flavonol branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: High-throughput RNA sequencing correlation with rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FeFLS2 was identified as a DEG strongly associated with rutin content. 2. As flavonol synthase, it produces flavonol aglycone precursor for rutin formation. 3. It is one of the key transcriptomic candidates highlighted for flavonoid biofortification.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review also mentions FeFLS in table formatting; likely flavonol synthase candidate(s).
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for selection or engineering to raise rutin content.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins, diverting flux away from flavonol/rutin formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as DEG associated with rutin content in stem, root and seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: High-throughput RNA sequencing correlation with rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FeDFR was identified among rutin-associated DEGs. 2. It occupies a branch point that can redirect flux toward anthocyanin-related products. 3. Its differential expression suggests importance in flavonoid partitioning relative to rutin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially modulates flux competition affecting rutin levels
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because review lists it as a DEG related to rutin content, though it may compete with rutin branch rather than directly synthesize rutin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target for rerouting flux toward rutin by downregulating competing branch activity.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression induced flavonoid biosynthesis genes and increased rutin biosynthesis up to 0.9 mg/g dry weight in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: transgenic overexpression in hairy root culture
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator used for metabolic engineering to enhance rutin production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy root transformation; overexpression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AtMYB12 in common buckwheat hairy roots induced flavonoid biosynthesis genes. 2. This transcriptional activation increased rutin biosynthesis. 3. The engineered hairy roots accumulated rutin up to 0.9 mg/g dry weight.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Heterologous regulator from Arabidopsis used as proof-of-concept engineering tool rather than native buckwheat gene.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transgenic regulator for flavonoid enhancement and pathway activation in buckwheat culture systems.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses expression of rutin biosynthetic pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with FtSAD2 for nuclear import
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a negative regulator of rutin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy root culture; protein interaction analysis; functional characterization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtMYB11 is an R2R3-MYB transcription factor that represses rutin accumulation. 2. It interacts with importin protein FtSAD2 through a SID-like motif. 3. FtSAD2 mediates its transport into the nucleus, enabling repression of rutin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction depends on two conserved Asp residues in the SID-like motif.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime target for knockout or attenuation to elevate rutin content.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB TF with conserved SID motif
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely nucleus; interacts with FtSAD2 for import
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses rutin biosynthetic pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-responsive negative regulator of rutin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene characterization in hairy root/transcription factor studies; interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtMYB13 is one of four JA-responsive MYB transcription factors reported in tartary buckwheat. 2. It is mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence. 3. It represses rutin biosynthesis and interacts with FtSAD2 via a conserved SID motif.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported together with FtMYB14/15/16 as repressors of rutin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate negative regulator for editing to increase rutin, especially in aerial tissues.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB TF with conserved SID motif
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely nucleus; interacts with FtSAD2 for import
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses rutin biosynthetic pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-responsive negative regulator of rutin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene characterization in hairy root/transcription factor studies; interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtMYB14 is a JA-responsive MYB transcription factor. 2. It is mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence. 3. It represses rutin biosynthesis and interacts with FtSAD2 through a conserved SID motif.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Negative regulator summarized from review of functional studies.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or suppressing FtMYB14 may support increased rutin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB TF with conserved SID motif
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely nucleus; interacts with FtSAD2 for import
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses rutin biosynthetic pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-responsive negative regulator of rutin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene characterization in hairy root/transcription factor studies; interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtMYB15 is a JA-responsive MYB transcription factor reported in tartary buckwheat. 2. It is mainly expressed in stem and inflorescence. 3. It represses rutin biosynthesis and interacts with FtSAD2 via conserved SID motif.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included with FtMYB13/14/16 in later functional reports summarized by the review.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for repression release to increase rutin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses rutin biosynthetic pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate-responsive signaling; nuclear import by Ftimportin-α1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Trafficked by Ftimportin-α1; no interaction with FtSAD2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Root-preferential JA-responsive repressor of rutin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtMYB16 is a JA-responsive MYB transcription factor mainly expressed in root. 2. It represses rutin biosynthesis. 3. Unlike FtMYB13/14/15, it lacks a SID motif, does not interact with FtSAD2, and is instead regulated through Ftimportin-α1-mediated nucleocytoplasmic trafficking.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinguished from other JA-responsive MYBs by absence of SID motif and different transport mechanism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prominent target for root-specific manipulation of rutin repression.
